questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The human eye is like a camera that has a lens with :
options: fixed focal length and fixed aperture size.
variable focal length and fixed aperture size.
fixed focal length and variable aperture size.
variable focal length and variable aperture size.
questions.solution:
The human eye functions similarly to a camera in many ways. When considering the characteristics of the eye's lens, we need to factor in its focal length and aperture size.

The focal length of the lens in the human eye is not fixed. The eye can change its focal length to focus on objects at different distances. This process is called accommodation, where the ciliary muscles adjust the shape of the lens to change its focal length.

Similarly, the aperture size of the human eye, which is the pupil, is variable. The size of the pupil changes in response to light intensity. In dim light, the pupil dilates to allow more light into the eye, and in bright light, it constricts to reduce the light entering the eye. This process is known as the pupillary light reflex.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : variable focal length and variable aperture size.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire at a point outside the wire depends
options: inversely on the distance from it
directly on the distance from it
inversely at short distances and directly at large distances from it
directly on the distance (at short distances) and inversely on the distance (at long distances) from it

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A point object is placed at the centre of curvature of a spherical concave mirror. Which one among the fol- lowing would be the correct location of image formed?
options: Between the focal point and the centre of curvature
questions.solution:
1. For a concave mirror of radius of curvature $R$, the focal length is

    $$f=\frac{R}{2}$$

  2. Mirror formula (Cartesian sign convention):

    $$\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{u}$$

    where

    • $u$ = object distance from pole (real object ⇒ $u>0$)

    • $v$ = image distance from pole (real image ⇒ $v>0$)

  3. Here the object is at the centre of curvature, so

    $$u=R$$

  4. Substitute into the mirror formula:

    $$\frac{1}{R/2}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{2}{R}=\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\frac{1}{v}=\frac{2}{R}-\frac{1}{R}=\frac{1}{R}$$

    $$\therefore v=R$$

  5. Hence the image is formed at a distance $R$ from the pole, i.e. at the centre of curvature.

Answer: Option 2 – At the centre of curvature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The work done by the force acting on an object is zero if the displacement of the object
options: is in the opposite direction of the direction of force
is in the same direction of the direction of force
is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force
questions.solution:
Work done is given by

$$ W = F s \cos \theta $$

where:

  • $F$ = force

  • $s$ = displacement

  • $\theta$ = angle between force and displacement

For work done to be zero,

$$ \cos \theta = 0 $$

This happens when

$$ \theta = 90^\circ $$

That means the displacement is perpendicular to the direction of force.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: is in perpendicular direction of the direction of force

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the current through an electrical machine running on direct current is $15 A$ and the machine runs for $10$ minutes, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is:
questions.solution:
To determine the charge that passes through the machine, we use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The formula that relates these quantities is given by :

$$ Q = I \times t $$

where :

$$ Q $$ is the charge (in coulombs, C)

$$ I $$ is the current (in amperes, A)

$$ t $$ is the time (in seconds, s)

We are given :

Current, $$ I = 15 \, \text{A} $$

Time, $$ t = 10 \, \text{minutes} $$

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds :

$$ 10 \, \text{minutes} \times 60 \, \text{seconds per minute} = 600 \, \text{seconds} $$

Now, substituting the given values into the formula, we get :

$$ Q = 15 \, \text{A} \times 600 \, \text{s} $$

$$ Q = 9000 \, \text{C} $$

Therefore, the charge that passes through the machine during this time is :

Option D : $$ 9000 \, \text{C} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field inside a long straight solenoid-carrying current
options: decreases as we move towards its end
increases as we move towards its end
questions.solution:
The magnetic field in an ideal, infinitely long, tightly‐wound solenoid is the same everywhere inside. In fact, by Ampère’s law one finds

$$B \,=\, \mu_0\,n\,I$$

where

• μ₀ is the permeability of free space,

• n is the number of turns per unit length, and

• I is the current through the winding.

Because this expression has no dependence on the position along the axis, the field is uniform inside. (Near the very ends of a real, finite solenoid the field does fall off, but for a “long” solenoid those edge effects are negligible.)

Answer: Option 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A sound wave has a frequency of 1 kHz and wavelength 50 cm. How long will it take to travel 1 km?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which of the following are the primary colours of light?
questions.solution:
The primary colors of light are identified based on the additive color model, where different colors of light are mixed to create other colors. In this model, the primary colors are:

Option B: Blue, Red, and Green

These colors can be combined in various ways to produce a wide spectrum of colors, including white when all are combined at full intensity. This model is the same principle used in devices like computer screens, televisions, and cameras, where different intensities of red, green, and blue light are combined to produce the wide array of colors we perceive.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following does not apply to sound waves in fluids?
options: They travel faster in liquids than in gases
questions.solution:
The option that does not apply to sound waves in fluids is:

Option C

They are transverse

Explanation:

Sound waves in fluids (liquids and gases) are longitudinal waves, not transverse waves. In longitudinal waves, the oscillations of the particles of the medium are parallel to the direction of wave propagation. This means that particles in the medium move back and forth in the same direction that the wave is traveling.

Let's break down the other options to see why they do apply to sound waves in fluids:

Option A: They transport energy

Sound waves indeed transport energy. As a sound wave propagates through a medium, it transfers energy from one particle to another through the medium.

Option B: They need a medium to travel

Sound waves require a medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to travel. They cannot propagate in a vacuum because there are no particles to transmit the vibrations.

Option D: They travel faster in liquids than in gases

Sound waves generally travel faster in liquids than in gases. This is because particles in liquids are more closely packed than in gases, allowing the sound waves to transmit vibrations more efficiently.

Therefore, Option C is the correct answer since sound waves in fluids are not transverse waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true for a simple harmonic oscillator?
options: Force acting is directly proportional to the displacement from the mean position and is in same direction.
Force acting is directly proportional to the displacement from the mean position and is in opposite direction
Acceleration of the oscillator is constant
The velocity of the oscillator is not periodic
questions.solution:
The force acting on a simple harmonic oscillator is described by Hooke's Law, which states that the force is proportional to the displacement from the equilibrium position and acts in the opposite direction. Mathematically, this is expressed as:

$$ F = -kx $$

where:

$ F $ is the restoring force,

$ k $ is the spring constant,

$ x $ is the displacement from the mean position.

This negative sign indicates that the force acts in the opposite direction of the displacement. Therefore, the correct statement is:

Option B: Force acting is directly proportional to the displacement from the mean position and is in opposite direction.

Other reasons why Option B is correct:

Option A contradicts the nature of restoring force in simple harmonic motion because the direction of force should be opposite to the displacement.

Option C is incorrect as the acceleration is not constant; it is maximum at maximum displacement and zero at the mean position.

Option D is incorrect since the velocity in simple harmonic motion is indeed periodic, following a sine or cosine function based on time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: There is a ball of mass 320 g. It has 625 J potential energy when released freely from a height. The speed with which it will hit the ground is
questions.solution:
First convert the mass to kilograms:

m = 320 g = 0.32 kg

All the potential energy turns into kinetic energy at the bottom:

$$\tfrac12\,m\,v^2 = \text{PE} = 625\text{ J}$$

Solving for v:

$$v = \sqrt{\frac{2\cdot625}{0.32}} = \sqrt{3906.25} = 62.5\text{ m/s}$$

So the ball hits the ground at 62.5 m/s (Option 1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following sketches correctly describes a lever of second class?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Lever of Second Class

A lever of the second class has three main parts:

  • The fulcrum is at one end. The fulcrum is the point where the lever pivots or turns.
  • The load (the thing you want to move or lift) is in the middle.
  • The effort (where you push or pull) is at the other end, opposite the fulcrum.

In a second-class lever:

  • The load is always between the fulcrum and where you apply effort.
  • The arm where you apply effort (effort arm) is always longer than the part holding the load (load arm), so you get a mechanical advantage greater than 1. This means it's easier to lift something.
  • Examples of second-class levers are wheelbarrows, nutcrackers, and bottle openers.

EXPLANATION:

When you look at sketches of different levers:

  • A second-class lever should always have:
    • Fulcrum at one end,
    • Load in the middle,
    • And effort at the other end.
  • The correct sketch must show the load between the fulcrum and the effort.

So, the sketch that correctly shows a lever of the second class is Option 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Ramesh cannot see distinctly objects kept beyond 2 m. This defect can be corrected by using a lens of power
questions.solution:
Ramesh's condition is known as myopia or nearsightedness, where he can't see objects clearly beyond a certain distance, in this case, 2 meters. To correct myopia, concave lenses (which have negative focal lengths) are typically used, because they help to diverge light rays before they reach the eye, thus moving the focal point back onto the retina.

We need to find the power of the lens required to correct his vision. The formula for the lens power $ P $ in diopters (D) is given by:

$$ P = \frac{1}{f} $$

where $ f $ is the focal length in meters. For Ramesh, the far point is at 2 meters, meaning the lens should have its focal length $ f $ set to -2 meters (negative because it's a concave lens). Substituting the focal length into the formula, we get:

$$ P = \frac{1}{-2 \text{ m}} = -0.5 \text{ D} $$

Therefore, the correct lens power needed to correct Ramesh's vision is -0.5 diopters.

So, the correct option is:

Option B: -0.5 D

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An object is placed between infinity and the pole (P) of a convex mirror. The position of the image is
options: between pole (P) and the focus (F), behind the mirror
between the focus (F) and infinity, behind the mirror
between the pole (P) and the infinity, in front of the mirror
at the focus (F), behind the mirror
questions.solution:
For a convex mirror the mirror‐formula

$$\frac1u + \frac1v = \frac1f$$

applies, with

$u>0$ (object in front of mirror),

$f<0$ (convex mirror focal length),

$v<0$ (image behind mirror).

If $u\to\infty$, then $1/v=1/f$ ⇒ $v=f$ (image at the focus, behind).

As $u$ decreases from ∞ toward 0, $\;1/v=1/f-1/u$ becomes more negative ⇒ $|v|$ decreases from $|f|$ toward 0.

So for any finite object distance the image lies between the pole $P$ and the focus $F$, behind the mirror.

Answer: Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following pairs of physical phenomenon and the discover is/are correctly matched?

 1. James Chadwick  Photoelectric
 2. Albert Einstein  Neutron
 3. Marie Curie  Radium
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The correct matching pair is "Marie Curie and Radium." Marie Curie, along with her husband Pierre Curie, discovered the element Radium. The other pairs are mismatched:

James Chadwick is known for discovering the neutron, not for the photoelectric effect.

Albert Einstein is recognized for his explanation of the photoelectric effect, not for discovering the neutron.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option D: 3 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, which one among the following is correct?
options: Red light deviates the most because red light has highest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has highest speed in prism
Red light deviates the most because red light has lowest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has lowest speed in prism
questions.solution:
In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, the different colors of light have different speeds when traveling through the prism. The speed of light in a medium is inversely related to the refractive index of that medium for that particular color. As a result, light with a shorter wavelength (like blue light) slows down more and, therefore, bends more than light with a longer wavelength (like red light) when it enters and exits the prism.

When white light passes through a prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to the difference in the refractive indices for different wavelengths. Blue light has a shorter wavelength compared to red light, which means it has a higher refractive index in the glass prism. Consequently, blue light deviates or bends more than red light.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D: Blue light deviates the most because blue light has the lowest speed in the prism.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
An iron nail sinks in water whereas an iron ship floats. Which of the following statements is correct in this regard?

1. Average density of ship is greater than that of the water

2. Average density of iron nail is greater than that of the water

3. Average density of the ship is less than that of the water

4. Average density of the ship is equal to that of the water

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To understand why an iron nail sinks in water while an iron ship floats, we need to consider the concept of density and buoyancy.

Density is defined as mass per unit volume, and it is given by the formula:

$$ \text{Density} = \frac{\text{Mass}}{\text{Volume}} $$

An iron nail has a high density because its mass is concentrated in a small volume, making its average density greater than that of water. As a result, it sinks.

On the other hand, an iron ship, despite being made of iron, is designed in such a way that it encloses a large volume of air. This design ensures that the overall (or average) density of the ship becomes less than that of water, enabling it to float. The ship's shape and structure cause it to displace a volume of water equal to its own weight before being submerged, allowing it to float due to the buoyant force that is equal to the weight of the displaced water.

Given these points, the correct statements are:

2. The average density of the iron nail is greater than that of water. (Since it sinks)

3. The average density of the ship is less than that of the water. (Since it floats)

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B

2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
A metallic bob X of mass m is released from position A. It collides elastically with another identical bob Y placed at rest at position B on a horizontal frictionless table. The angle AOB is 30°.

How high does the bob X rise immediately after the collision?
options: To the same height as that of position A on the other side in the same trajectory
To half the height as that of position A on the other side along the same trajectory
The same height at position A
questions.solution:
To solve this problem, we need to consider the principles of conservation of momentum and conservation of energy. The collision between the two bobs is elastic, meaning both kinetic energy and momentum are conserved.

First, let's denote the initial height from which the bob X is released as $$h$$. When bob X is released from position A, it converts its potential energy into kinetic energy just before the collision :

$$mgh = \frac{1}{2}mv^2$$

Solving for the velocity $$v$$ just before collision :

$$v = \sqrt{2gh}$$

Since the bobs are identical (same mass $$m$$) and the collision is elastic, the velocities of the bobs after the collision can be derived using the following properties of an elastic collision: the moving object will come to rest, and the object at rest will move with the velocity of the moving object. Therefore, after the collision, the bob Y will move with the initial velocity of bob X, and bob X will come to a stop.

Because bob X comes to a stop immediately after the collision, it does not rise to any height after the collision. Hence, the correct answer is :

Option D : It stops at position B

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: According to the New Cartesian Sign Convention, which one of the following is correct is respect of the formula $\dfrac{1}{f} = \dfrac{1}{v}+ \dfrac{1}{u}$, where symbols have their usual meanings?
options: It applies only to spherical mirrors
It applies only to spherical lenses
It applies to spherical mirrors as well as spherical lenses
questions.solution:
In the New Cartesian Sign Convention, the formula for a spherical mirror is

$ \frac{1}{f} \;=\; \frac{1}{u} \;+\; \frac{1}{v}, $

where $f$ is the focal length of the mirror, $u$ is the object distance, and $v$ is the image distance.

However, for a thin spherical lens, the corresponding formula (under the same sign convention) is

$ \frac{1}{f} \;=\; \frac{1}{v} \;-\; \frac{1}{u}. $

Thus, the given formula

$ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u} $

holds only for spherical mirrors, and not for lenses under the New Cartesian Sign Convention. Therefore, the correct choice is:

Option A: It applies only to spherical mirrors.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: At uniform speed the acceleration is
questions.solution:
Option 3: Zero

Since acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, for uniform speed (and straight-line motion) we have

$$a = \frac{dv}{dt} = 0\,. $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The presence of magnetic field can be determined using which one of the following instruments?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Magnetic needle. Here's why:

A magnetic needle, often found in a compass, is specifically designed to align itself with the magnetic field lines of the Earth. This alignment is the basis of how compasses work.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: Ammeter - An ammeter measures the flow of electric current, not magnetic fields.
  • Option B: Voltmeter - A voltmeter measures electrical potential difference, not magnetic fields.
  • Option D: Motor - A motor uses a magnetic field to produce rotation, but it doesn't directly measure the presence of a magnetic field.

In summary, a magnetic needle is the most direct way to detect the presence of a magnetic field because it physically responds to the field's influence.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A 5 N force is defined when a mass of 10 kg is accelerated with

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A system that does NOT allow exchange of heat with its surrounding is called
questions.solution:
Explanation:

Adiabatic Process:

An adiabatic process occurs in a thermodynamic system when there is no exchange of heat between the system and its surroundings. This means that throughout the adiabatic process, the system insulates itself against heat transfer.

Mathematically, this is represented by $ ΔQ = 0 $, indicating no heat transfer.

Additional Information:

Isothermal Process: In this process, the system undergoes changes such that its temperature remains constant.

Isobaric Process: This involves changes where the pressure remains constant.

Isochoric Process: Here, the system's volume remains constant during the change.

Isentropic Process:

In an isentropic process, the entropy of the system remains constant. It is also referred to as a constant-entropy process.

This process is both reversible and adiabatic, meaning no heat or matter is transferred.

It is an idealized concept in thermodynamics, often serving as a model to aspire to.

Entropy:

Entropy measures the amount of thermal energy in a system that cannot be used to perform work, per unit of temperature.

Over time, the entropy of the world tends to increase as systems move towards less equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: All objects experience a buoyancy when they are immersed in a fluid. Buoyancy is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A glass prism splits white light into different colours. This phenomenon is called dispersion of light by prism. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Red light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light
Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the reflection of light
questions.solution:
Option B is correct: Violet light will deviate the most and it is because of the refraction of light.

When white light passes through a glass prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to refraction. Each color of light bends at a different angle because each color has a different wavelength and refractive index. Violet light, having the shortest wavelength, is refracted the most, while red light, having the longest wavelength, is refracted the least. This differential bending of light waves causes the splitting of white light into its spectral colors, known as dispersion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about dihydrogen (H2) is not correct?
options: H<sub>2</sub> is lighter than air and insoluble in water.
H<sub>2</sub> is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy.
H<sub>2</sub> reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides.
A mixture of NO<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub> is known as Syngas.
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding dihydrogen (H₂):

Option A: "H₂ is lighter than air and insoluble in water."

Correct. Hydrogen is indeed lighter than air and has very low solubility in water.

Option B: "H₂ is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy."

Correct. The H-H bond in H₂ is very strong (with a high dissociation enthalpy, approximately 436 kJ/mol), which makes the molecule relatively unreactive (or inert) under standard conditions unless a catalyst or extreme conditions are provided.

Option C: "H₂ reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides."

Correct. Alkali metals can react with hydrogen gas at elevated temperatures to form corresponding metal hydrides (e.g., sodium hydride, NaH).

Option D: "A mixture of NO₂ and H₂ is known as Syngas."

Not Correct. Syngas, short for synthesis gas, is typically a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). A mixture of nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) and H₂ does not constitute syngas.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is Option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the correct composition of Borax?
questions.solution:
Borax, also known as sodium borate, is a mineral and a salt of boric acid. Its chemical composition includes sodium (Na), boron (B), oxygen (O), and hydrogen (H). The correct formula for Borax is $$\text{Na}_2\text{B}_4\text{O}_7\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$.

Given the options, the correct composition of Borax is:

Option B

Sodium, Boron, Oxygen and Hydrogen

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct order of the valencies of elements Ne, Si, N and Mg?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals floats in cold water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:

LIST I

(Process)

LIST II

(Separation method)

A.

Separation of acetone and water from their mixture

1.

Chromatography

B.

Separation of water
and kerosene oil
from their mixture

2.

Centrifugation

C.

Separation of cream from milk

3.

Distillation

D.

Separation of pigments from plant extract

4.

Separating Funnel
questions.solution:
Here's a breakdown of the separation methods and why they match with the given processes:

A. Separation of acetone and water from their mixture:

Correct Answer: 3. Distillation

Acetone and water are miscible liquids (they dissolve in each other). Distillation takes advantage of their different boiling points. Acetone has a lower boiling point than water, so it will evaporate first when the mixture is heated. The vapor is then collected and condensed back into a liquid, separating it from the water.

B. Separation of water and kerosene oil from their mixture:

Correct Answer: 4. Separating Funnel

Water and kerosene oil are immiscible liquids (they don't mix). A separating funnel allows for the separation based on density. Kerosene is less dense than water, so it will float on top. The heavier water is drained out from the bottom of the funnel, leaving the kerosene oil behind.

C. Separation of cream from milk:

Correct Answer: 2. Centrifugation

Centrifugation uses centrifugal force to separate components with different densities. Cream is less dense than the rest of the milk. When spun rapidly, the heavier milk settles at the bottom, while the lighter cream rises to the top, allowing for easy separation.

D. Separation of pigments from plant extract:

Correct Answer: 1. Chromatography

Chromatography is used to separate different components of a mixture based on their affinity for a stationary phase and a mobile phase. Plant extracts often contain multiple pigments with different properties. Chromatography allows for the separation of these pigments by their varying rates of movement through the stationary phase.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option C: A - 3, B - 4, C - 2, D - 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a solution?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is known as Milk of Magnesia?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Magnesium hydroxide.

Magnesium hydroxide is commonly known as Milk of Magnesia. It has the chemical formula $$\text{Mg(OH)}_2$$. This compound forms a suspension in water that appears milky, which is why it gets the name "Milk of Magnesia." It is often used as an antacid to neutralize stomach acid and as a laxative to relieve constipation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists 

List I

(Element)

List II

(Atomicity) 

A.

 Phosphorus 

1.

 2

B.

 Nitrogen

2.

 1

C.

Neon

3.

8

D.

Sulphur

4.

4
questions.solution:
We match each element with its atomicity in its elemental form:

  • Phosphorus exists as $P_4$

    So, atomicity $= 4$

  • Nitrogen exists as $N_2$

    So, atomicity $= 2$

  • Neon is a noble gas and exists as single atoms

    So, atomicity $= 1$

  • Sulphur exists as $S_8$

    So, atomicity $= 8$

Now match with List II:

  • A. Phosphorus $\rightarrow 4$ $\rightarrow$ 4

  • B. Nitrogen $\rightarrow 2$ $\rightarrow$ 1

  • C. Neon $\rightarrow 1$ $\rightarrow$ 2

  • D. Sulphur $\rightarrow 8$ $\rightarrow$ 3

So the correct matching is:

$$ A - 4,\quad B - 1,\quad C - 2,\quad D - 3 $$

Hence, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: An iron nail dipped in copper sulphate solution turns brown. This is due to which one of the following types of reactions?
questions.solution:
The iron nail turning brown when dipped in copper sulphate solution is due to the following reaction:

$$ \text{Fe (s)} + \text{CuSO}_4 \text{(aq)} \rightarrow \text{FeSO}_4 \text{(aq)} + \text{Cu (s)} $$

In this reaction, iron displaces copper from copper sulphate solution, forming iron sulphate and depositing copper metal on the nail, which is observed as a brown layer.

Thus, the correct type of reaction is:

Option D: Displacement reaction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following acid is used by goldsmith for cleaning of gold and silver articles?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Nitric acid. Here's why:

Goldsmiths use nitric acid to clean gold and silver articles because of its unique properties:

  • Reacts with impurities: Nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent. It reacts with common impurities found in gold and silver, such as copper, silver sulfide, and other metals. This reaction forms soluble salts, which can be easily washed away, leaving the precious metals clean.
  • Does not react with gold (in its pure form): While nitric acid is a strong acid, it doesn't readily react with pure gold. This is why it's effective for cleaning gold without damaging it.
  • Formation of Aqua Regia: When nitric acid is mixed with hydrochloric acid in a specific ratio, it creates a highly corrosive mixture known as Aqua Regia. Aqua Regia can dissolve gold, but goldsmiths typically use nitric acid alone for cleaning purposes.

The other options are not suitable for cleaning gold and silver:

  • Sulphuric acid (Option A): While sulphuric acid is a strong acid, it can react with both gold and silver, potentially damaging the articles.
  • Hydrochloric acid (Option C): Hydrochloric acid is less effective at removing impurities compared to nitric acid. It also reacts with silver, making it unsuitable for cleaning silver articles.
  • Phosphoric acid (Option D): Phosphoric acid is a weak acid and generally not strong enough to dissolve the impurities found in gold and silver.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: What is the action of litmus on ethanol?
options: Ethanol turns blue litmus to red; confirming acidic nature of ethanol
Ethanol turns red litmus to blue; confirming basic nature of ethanol
questions.solution:
Litmus is a commonly used indicator that helps in determining whether a substance is acidic or basic. Ethanol (C2H5OH) is an alcohol and is generally considered to be a neutral substance. It does not display acidic or basic properties like acids or bases do. Additionally, ethanol does not have a bleaching action.

When litmus paper, whether blue or red, is introduced to ethanol, there is no color change. This indicates that ethanol is neutral towards litmus paper. Therefore, the action of litmus on ethanol can be described by the following option:

Option A: Litmus is neutral towards ethanol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following is an example of chemical change?
questions.solution:
Mixing NaOH and HCl is a chemical change. In that reaction new substances form (salt and water):

$$\text{NaOH} + \text{HCl} \;\longrightarrow\; \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

Quick check of the other options:

• Sublimation of camphor – physical (phase change)

• Melting of ice – physical (phase change)

• Heating an iron rod – physical (thermal expansion/colour change)

So the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct reactivity series with water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following compounds has a sweet and fruity smell?
questions.solution:
Among the given compounds, the one that has a sweet and fruity smell is Ethyl acetate.

Option A: Ethyl alcohol (ethanol) has a characteristic smell often associated with alcoholic beverages and is not typically described as fruity.

Option B: Acetic acid has a strong, pungent vinegar-like smell.

Option C: Ethyl acetate is known for its sweet, fruity aroma and is commonly used in perfumes, as well as in flavorings and as a solvent. Its molecular formula is $$\text{CH}_3\text{COOCH}_2\text{CH}_3$$ and it is an ester, which are often known for their pleasant, fruity smells.

Option D: Acetophenone has a sweet but more intense smell, often described as reminiscent of almonds or cherries, but not typically purely fruity.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: Ethyl acetate

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Dry ice is used on a performing stage to produce mist in air. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option A: sublimation. Here's why:

Sublimation is the process where a substance transitions directly from a solid phase to a gaseous phase, bypassing the liquid phase.

Here's how it applies to dry ice:

  • Dry ice is frozen carbon dioxide (CO2).

  • At room temperature and standard atmospheric pressure, CO2 exists as a gas.

  • When dry ice is exposed to air, it absorbs heat energy from the surroundings.

  • This heat energy causes the CO2 molecules in the dry ice to vibrate faster and transition directly from a solid to a gas.

This gaseous CO2 is what creates the mist or fog effect on stage. The mist is not actually water vapor, but tiny droplets of liquid water that condense around the cold CO2 gas.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Evaporation: Involves a transition from liquid to gas (like water boiling). Dry ice skips the liquid phase.

  • Condensation: Is the opposite of evaporation (gas to liquid), like dew forming on grass.

  • Precipitation: Involves water falling from the atmosphere as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following statements is not correct with respect to allotropes of Carbon?
options: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity.
Diamond is the hardest substance known.
Fullerene is one of the allotropes of carbon.
Next to diamond, graphite is the second hardest known substance.
questions.solution:
The statement which is not correct with respect to allotropes of Carbon is:

Option D: Next to diamond, graphite is the second hardest known substance.

Explanation:

Option A is correct: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity. This is because graphite has free electrons that can move through its layers.

Option B is correct: Diamond is the hardest substance known. Diamonds are renowned for their hardness.

Option C is correct: Fullerene is one of the allotropes of carbon. Fullerenes are molecules made entirely of carbon, taking the form of hollow spheres, ellipsoids, or tubes.

Option D is incorrect: Graphite is not the second hardest known substance. In fact, it is quite soft and slippery, used as a lubricant and in pencils for writing due to its layered structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Mica is mainly used in:
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option D: electrical and electronic industries.

Mica is predominantly used in the electrical and electronic industries due to its excellent insulating properties and high dielectric strength. It is commonly found in components such as capacitors, insulators, and various other electrical equipment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For an element with atomic number 35, which one of the following will be the correct number of electrons in its valence shell based on Bohr's model of an atom?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the colour of hydrogen gas?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: A scale measuring hydrogen ion concentration in a solution is called pH scale
The higher the hydrogen ion concentration in a solution, higher is its pH
We can measure pH generally from 0 to 14 on a pH scale
The 'p' in pH stands for 'potenz' in German meaning power
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 2.

Explanation:

By definition,

$$\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+].$$

If $[H^+]$ increases, $\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes up, so $\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes down.

Hence, a higher hydrogen‐ion concentration actually means a lower pH, not a higher one.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements regarding oxidation and reduction reactions is NOT correct?
options: If a substance loses hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance loses oxygen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains hydrogen during reaction, it is reduced
If a substance gains oxygen during reaction, it is oxidized
questions.solution:
The statement that’s NOT correct is Option 1.

Here's why:

Oxidation involves either gaining O₂ or losing H₂ (loss of electrons).

Reduction involves either losing O₂ or gaining H₂ (gain of electrons).

So losing hydrogen is an oxidation process, not a reduction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass number of argon is 40. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The number of protons in argon is 22.
The number of neutrons in argon is 18.
The number of electrons in argon is 18.
The sum of numbers of protons and electrons in argon is 40.
